name: Rustic Herbed Apricot Chicken
recipeIngredient: 2 Chickens, cut into 8 pieces each, wing tips trimmed
1 large Onion, halved and thinly sliced
Freshly ground black pepper to taste
1 tbsp Chopped flat-leaf parsley, for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the chicken pieces and pat dry.
2. In a large bowl, combine 1/4 cup olive oil, chopped garlic, vinegar, thyme, tarragon, and 1/2 teaspoon pepper. Add the chicken, toss together well, and marinate at room temperature for 2-3 hours or refrigerate overnight.
3. Remove the chicken from the marinade, scraping off any remaining bits. Reserve the marinade. Heat the remaining 2 tablespoons of oil in a large, heavy casserole. Brown the chicken in small batches over medium heat until golden, turning once or twice. Set finished pieces aside.
4. Add onion slices to the casserole and reduce heat. Cook, stirring for 2 minutes or until they begin to brown. Toss onion slices with flour and continue cooking, stirring constantly, for 2 minutes longer.
5. Add broth, port wine, and red currant jelly. Cook, stirring, for another 2 minutes. Stir in the apricots, reserved marinade, and chicken.
6. Cook over low heat, partially covered, for 20 minutes. Remove the cover and cook for 20-30 minutes more or until the chicken is cooked through. Season with salt and pepper to taste. If desired, serve with cooked noodles and garnish with chopped parsley.
Per serving (without noodles): 555 calories, 25 grams fat, 124 milligrams cholesterol.
